concepts.name: Linear Relations
concepts.description: Represent, compare, and interpret linear relationships using tables, graphs, equations, slope/rate of change, and initial values.

concepts.name: Powers and Scientific Notation
concepts.description: Evaluate powers, understand exponent patterns and laws, and represent very large or very small numbers using scientific notation.

concepts.name: Rational Numbers and Fractions
concepts.description: Use integers, fractions, mixed numbers, ratios, rates, percentages, and proportions in mathematical and real-world contexts.

concepts.name: Box Plots and Scatter Plots
concepts.description: Represent and analyse one-variable and two-variable data using box plots, quartiles, scatter plots, correlation, regression, and predictions.

concepts.name: Right Triangles and Measurement
concepts.description: Solve measurement and geometry problems involving unit conversions, dimension changes, right-triangle side lengths, composite shapes, and volume relationships.

concepts.name: Financial Literacy: Interest Rates
concepts.description: Compare financial decisions involving interest rates, borrowing time, compound interest, down payments, appreciation, depreciation, and budgets.

concepts.name: Quadratic Modelling and Graph Features
concepts.description: Represent and interpret quadratic situations using data, parabolas, and key graph features such as the vertex, zeros, and axis of symmetry.

concepts.name: Transformations of Quadratic Graphs
concepts.description: Investigate and interpret how transformations affect quadratic graphs and connect vertex-form equations to graphical changes.

concepts.name: Expanding and Factoring Quadratics
concepts.description: Expand, simplify, and factor quadratic expressions, and connect algebraic factors to x-intercepts on graphs.

concepts.name: Solving Quadratic Equations and Applications
concepts.description: Solve quadratic equations using multiple methods and apply quadratic reasoning to realistic problems.

concepts.name: Solving Linear Systems
concepts.description: Solve systems of two linear equations using algebraic methods and apply them to realistic contexts.

concepts.name: Distance, Midpoint, and Circles
concepts.description: Use coordinate geometry to work with midpoint, distance, and circles centred at the origin.

concepts.name: Analytic Geometry Proof and Verification
concepts.description: Verify geometric properties on the coordinate plane using slope, distance, midpoint, and multi-step analytic-geometry reasoning.

concepts.name: Similarity and Congruence
concepts.description: Compare and apply similarity and congruence relationships in triangles and other geometric figures.

concepts.name: Right-Triangle Trigonometry
concepts.description: Use sine, cosine, tangent, and the Pythagorean theorem to solve right-triangle problems in mathematical and applied contexts.

concepts.name: Sine Law, Cosine Law, and Acute-Triangle Problem Solving
concepts.description: Develop and apply the sine law and cosine law to solve side-length and angle problems in acute triangles.

concepts.name: Similar Triangles and Proportional Reasoning
concepts.description: Use proportional reasoning and similar triangles to compare side lengths and solve measurement problems in realistic contexts.

concepts.name: Right-Triangle Trigonometry Applications
concepts.description: Use sine, cosine, tangent, and the Pythagorean theorem to solve right-triangle problems in practical settings.

concepts.name: Surface Area, Volume, and Measurement Systems
concepts.description: Solve surface-area, volume, and unit-conversion problems using both metric and imperial measurement systems.

concepts.name: Solving First-Degree Equations and Formulas
concepts.description: Solve first-degree equations and rearrange formulas to isolate and determine unknown quantities.

concepts.name: Graphing Lines and Linear Equations
concepts.description: Connect slope, rate of change, graphs, and equations of lines, and write line equations from different forms of information.

concepts.name: Linear Systems and Real-World Applications
concepts.description: Solve systems of linear equations graphically or algebraically and interpret the solutions in realistic situations.

concepts.name: Expanding and Factoring Quadratics
concepts.description: Expand and factor simple quadratic expressions, including common-factor forms, trinomials, and differences of squares.

concepts.name: Quadratic Graph Features and Representations
concepts.description: Identify key graph features of parabolas and connect graphs of quadratic relations to standard and factored forms.

concepts.name: Interpreting Quadratic Graphs in Applications
concepts.description: Solve application problems by interpreting the key features of quadratic graphs from equations, experiments, and technology-generated models.

concepts.name: Ecosystem Sustainability and Biodiversity
concepts.description: Investigate interactions among Earth's spheres, biodiversity, soil and water quality, succession, and other factors that support sustainable ecosystems.

concepts.name: Climate Change, Communities, and Action
concepts.description: Examine how climate change affects ecosystems and communities, including First Nations, Metis, and Inuit communities, and evaluate ways to mitigate those impacts.

concepts.name: Cycling of Matter, Energy Flow, and Sustainable Agriculture
concepts.description: Explore matter cycles, energy flow, photosynthesis, cellular respiration, and agricultural practices that support sustainable ecosystems.

concepts.name: Atomic Structure and Periodic Table Patterns
concepts.description: Connect atomic models, subatomic particles, atomic structure, and periodic-table organization to patterns in element properties.

concepts.name: Elements, Compounds, and Chemical Technologies
concepts.description: Study physical and chemical properties of elements and compounds, their use in products and technologies, and their impacts on society and the environment.

concepts.name: Static Electricity and Electric Charges
concepts.description: Investigate electric charges, conductivity, and how static and current electricity relate to subatomic particles and atomic structure.

concepts.name: Circuits, Current, Voltage, and Resistance
concepts.description: Build and analyse circuits to understand current, potential difference, resistance, and mathematical relationships in series and parallel circuits.

concepts.name: Electrical Energy, Efficiency, and Sustainability
concepts.description: Assess electrical energy production, consumption, efficiency, and conservation, including sustainable practices and emerging technologies.

concepts.name: Solar System, Universe, and Astronomical Phenomena
concepts.description: Investigate the Sun, solar-system objects, the universe, relative distances, and astronomical phenomena observable from Earth.

concepts.name: Space Exploration and Earth Applications
concepts.description: Evaluate the impacts of space exploration and the ways space-observation and space-exploration technologies help us understand Earth and support sustainable practices.

expectations.expectation_text: Research a number concept, describe its cultural development and use, and connect it to a current context.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Mathematics, Grade 9, De-streamed (MTH1W), 2021

expectations.expectation_text: Describe how subsets of a number system are defined and compare their similarities and differences.

expectations.expectation_text: Use patterns and number relationships to explain density, infinity, and limits in number sets.

expectations.expectation_text: Analyse how exponent sign and size affect powers, express numbers in scientific notation, and evaluate powers.

expectations.expectation_text: Explain how positive and negative signs affect ratios, rates, fractions, and decimals in context.

expectations.expectation_text: Solve problems using operations with positive and negative fractions and mixed numbers, including formulas, measurement, and linear relations.

expectations.expectation_text: Pose and solve problems involving rates, percentages, and proportions across data, measurement, geometry, linear relations, and financial contexts.

expectations.expectation_text: Research an algebraic concept, describe its cultural development and use, and connect it to a current context.

expectations.expectation_text: Create algebraic expressions that generalize relationships shown in words, numbers, visuals, and contexts.

expectations.expectation_text: Compare algebraic expressions using concrete, numerical, graphical, and algebraic methods to identify equivalent expressions and justify choices.

expectations.expectation_text: Simplify algebraic expressions by applying properties of operations with numbers, representations, and tools.

expectations.expectation_text: Create and solve equations for varied contexts and verify solutions.

expectations.expectation_text: Use coding to demonstrate algebraic concepts such as variables, parameters, equations, and inequalities.

expectations.expectation_text: Create code by decomposing situations into computational steps to represent mathematical relationships and solve problems.

expectations.expectation_text: Read code to predict outcomes and alter constraints, parameters, and outputs for similar or new mathematical situations.

expectations.expectation_text: Compare graph shapes for linear and non-linear relations, describe rates of change, connect to patterns, and make predictions.

expectations.expectation_text: Represent linear relations with concrete materials, tables, graphs, and equations, and connect representations to rates of change and initial values.

expectations.expectation_text: Compare two linear relations in the form y = ax + b graphically and algebraically, and interpret their intersection in context.

expectations.expectation_text: Compare characteristics of graphs, tables, and equations for linear and non-linear relations.

expectations.expectation_text: Identify a current big-data context and describe implications and consequences of collecting, storing, representing, and using the data.

expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Measurement and Trigonometry

expectations.expectation_text: Describe, through participation in an activity or investigation, how trigonometry is applied in an occupation.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Measurement and Trigonometry

expectations.expectation_text: Use the imperial system when solving measurement problems involving practical contexts such as lumber, carpeting, soil, or concrete.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Measurement and Trigonometry

expectations.expectation_text: Perform everyday conversions within and between the metric and imperial systems to solve measurement problems.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Measurement and Trigonometry

expectations.expectation_text: Determine the relationship for calculating the surface area of a pyramid through investigation.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Measurement and Trigonometry

expectations.expectation_text: Solve problems involving surface areas of prisms, pyramids, and cylinders, and volumes of prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres, including composite figures.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Measurement and Trigonometry

expectations.expectation_text: Solve first-degree equations involving one variable, including equations with fractional coefficients.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Determine the value of a first-degree variable from a formula by isolating the variable or substituting known values first.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Express the equation of a line in the form y = mx + b when given a form such as Ax + By + C = 0.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Connect the rate of change of a linear relation to the slope of a line and define slope as rise over run.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Identify y = mx + b as a common equation form for a straight line and recognize the special cases x = a and y = b.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Identify the geometric significance of m and b in the equation y = mx + b through investigation with technology.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate properties of slopes of lines and line segments, including direction, positive or negative rate of change, steepness, and parallelism.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Graph lines by hand using techniques such as slope and y-intercept or x- and y-intercepts.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Determine the equation of a line from its graph, from slope and y-intercept, from slope and a point, or from two points.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Determine graphically the point of intersection of two linear relations.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Solve systems of two linear equations with integral coefficients using substitution or elimination.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Solve realistic problems represented by systems of two linear equations by choosing an appropriate algebraic or graphical method.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Modelling Linear Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Expand and simplify quadratic expressions involving one variable that are products of two binomials or squares of binomials.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Factor binomials and trinomials involving one variable up to degree two by determining a common factor.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Factor simple trinomials of the form x2 + bx + c.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Factor differences of squares of the form x2 - a2.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Collect or use data that can be represented by a quadratic relation, graph the data, and draw a curve of best fit when appropriate.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Determine through investigation with technology that a quadratic relation of the form y = ax2 + bx + c is represented by a parabola and has a constant second difference.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Identify the key features of a parabola, including axis of symmetry, vertex, y-intercept, zeros, and maximum or minimum value, from a graph or technology-generated graph.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Compare the graphs of y = x2 + bx + c and y = (x - r)(x - s), and connect each form to features such as y-intercepts and x-intercepts.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Solve problems involving a quadratic relation by interpreting a given graph or a graph generated with technology from its equation.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.strand_name: Quadratic Relations of the Form y = ax2 + bx + c
expectations.expectation_text: Solve problems by interpreting the significance of key features of graphs obtained from experimental data involving quadratic relations.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grades 9 and 10: Mathematics, 2005
expectations.source_page_or_section: Foundations of Mathematics, Grade 10, Applied - Quadratic Relations

expectations.expectation_text: Assess how climate change affects local and global ecosystem sustainability, examine initiatives that combat climate change, and identify further solutions.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Assess how climate change affects communities in Canada, including First Nations, Metis, and Inuit communities.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate and explain how sustainable practices used by various communities, including First Nations, Metis, and Inuit communities, reflect ecosystem understanding.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate interactions among the biosphere, hydrosphere, lithosphere, and atmosphere, and explain why these interactions matter for ecosystem sustainability.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Explain how naturally occurring processes, including the cycling of matter and the flow of energy, help maintain dynamic equilibrium within and between ecosystems.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Compare cellular respiration and photosynthesis, and explain how their complementary relationship helps maintain ecosystem equilibrium.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate biodiversity, air and water quality, soil health, succession, and related factors, and explain how they support ecosystem sustainability.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Explain how various human activities affect the dynamic equilibrium of ecosystems.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Use climate-change indicators to describe impacts on local and global ecosystems, and analyse how human activities contribute to climate change.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Explain how sustainable practices involving matter cycling and energy flow can be applied in agricultural innovations.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Assess social, environmental, and economic impacts across the life cycle of consumer products, considering the elements and compounds used to make them.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Analyse impacts of emerging chemical technologies in different fields, including skilled trades, and assess factors that influence their development.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate properties, changes, and interactions of matter that are important for ecosystem sustainability and dynamic equilibrium.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Research how experimental evidence shaped different atomic models, and compare and contrast those models.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Identify the location, relative mass, and charge of subatomic particles in atoms using the Bohr-Rutherford model.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Explain how an element's position in the periodic table relates to the structure of its atoms, using models.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate physical and chemical properties of elements to classify them, relate them to periodic-table organization, and identify patterns.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate and describe physical and chemical properties of elements and compounds, including substances in common household products.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Describe how the structure of simple compounds is related to their chemical formulas.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Assess social, environmental, and economic benefits and challenges of producing electrical energy from different sources.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Evaluate how electrical energy production and consumption affect communities locally or globally, and describe ways to achieve sustainable practices.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Develop a plan of action to address a local or global issue related to electrical energy production or consumption, including energy-conservation strategies.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Analyse social, environmental, and economic impacts of emerging technologies related to electrical energy production, storage, consumption, and conservation.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Conduct investigations to explain how electric charges behave in static and current electricity, and relate that behaviour to subatomic particles and atomic structure.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Determine the conductivity of materials by investigating their ability to hold or transfer electric charges.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Identify the components of a direct-current circuit, explain their functions, and identify electrical quantities, symbols, and SI units.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Investigate relationships between electric current, potential difference, and resistance in circuits, and develop a mathematical model for those relationships.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Apply a mathematical model to calculate electric current, potential difference, and resistance in real-world situations.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Construct series and parallel circuits to compare electric current, potential difference, and resistance in both types of circuits.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Explain the difference between electricity and electrical energy.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Determine the efficiency of electrical devices that consume or produce electrical energy, and identify the energy transformations involved.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Evaluate social, environmental, and economic impacts of space observation and exploration.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Evaluate how space-observation and space-exploration technologies help us understand climate change, natural disasters, and other phenomena.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Assess ways technologies related to space observation and exploration are applied in different fields, including their support for sustainable practices on Earth.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Describe the importance of the Sun and its characteristics, including its role in the solar system and in sustaining life on Earth.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Explain how the Sun's energy causes natural phenomena on Earth and contributes to renewable energy production.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Summarize observational evidence used to support theories about the origin and evolution of the universe and the solar system, considering diverse ways of knowing.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Describe major components of the solar system and the universe, and compare their characteristics.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Quantify distances in the solar system and the universe using relative distances, sizes, and appropriate units of measure.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022

expectations.expectation_text: Conduct investigations to explain causes of astronomical phenomena that can be observed from Earth.
expectations.source_document: The Ontario Curriculum, Grade 9: Science, 2022
